message:
fix(task_handle): prevent spurious cancel when dropping ready JoinFuture

When a JoinFuture was dropped without being polled (e.g., the caller
created it but never awaited it), the Drop impl would unconditionally
write a cancellation reason even if the task had already completed
and its result was sitting in the oneshot channel. This caused:

- Misleading `cancel_requested` state on already-finished tasks
- Incorrect `cancel_reason` overwriting on Cx inner state
- False positives in supervision logs that monitor cancel events

Add an `is_finished()` guard at the top of JoinFuture::drop to skip
the abort path entirely when the result is already available. The
existing cancel-on-drop semantics are preserved for genuinely
in-progress tasks.

- Add test `drop_join_does_not_abort_if_result_already_ready` that
  pre-fills a oneshot channel, creates a TaskHandle, immediately drops
  the join future, and asserts neither cancel_requested nor
  cancel_reason are set on the Cx inner state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
